title: Bulbophyllum hornense
intro: Bulbophyllum hornense, also known as Horn's Bulbophyllum, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, with its health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum hornense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bulbophyllum hornense is often prepared as a decoction or infusions, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum hornense is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Bulbophyllum. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is further divided into the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification places Bulbophyllum hornense within the tribe Dendrobieae and the subtribe Dendrobiinae. The species is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia and is characterized by its unique floral structure. Further classification at the species level is based on its distinctive morphological features and reproductive patterns.

It is believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in wound healing and other health benefits.
